description: Multi-model /team-review with 2 native teammates (code-reviewer, security-reviewer via mailbox bridge) and 2 external adapters (codex, gemini). Security finding raised by 1 native team source + 1 external. Verifies cross-source dedup with source_type attribution preserved (FR-MMT3, FR-MMT20).

raw_findings.title: Session token uses Math.random() — insufficient entropy
raw_findings.description: Session token is generated via Math.random(). Predictable; allows session hijacking via token guessing.

raw_findings.title: Session tokens generated with non-cryptographic RNG
raw_findings.description: createSession uses Math.random() for token bytes. Must use crypto.randomBytes() or webcrypto.getRandomValues(). Critical — token predictability breaks the entire auth model.

raw_findings.title: No session token rotation on privilege change
raw_findings.description: When a user's role escalates, the session token is reused. Best practice: rotate tokens on privilege boundary crossings.

findings.title: Session tokens generated with non-cryptographic RNG
findings.description: createSession uses Math.random() for token bytes. Must use crypto.randomBytes() or webcrypto.getRandomValues(). Critical — token predictability breaks the entire auth model.
findings.consolidation_notes: Severity reconciled high → critical (1-level diff, max). Security category exempt from minority-of-one demotion. Cross-source attribution preserved: 1 native-team + 1 external.

findings.title: No session token rotation on privilege change
findings.description: When a user's role escalates, the session token is reused. Best practice: rotate tokens on privilege boundary crossings.
findings.consolidation_notes: Minority-of-one demotion applied: medium → low (single source, non-security category, medium confidence).

consolidated_output.summary: 1 CRITICAL security finding (session token entropy) raised cross-source by native team + codex; 1 LOW maintainability finding (token rotation) raised by code-reviewer alone (demoted).
